sglang.title: [NPU] torch_npu profiler tensorboard path type fix
sglang.author: mengchengTang
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The torch_npu.profiler.tensorboard_trace_handler strictly requires a string type for the dir_name argument. However, in SchedulerProfilerMixin, self.torch_profiler_output_dir is converted to a pathlib.Path object during initialization (init_profile). This type mismatch causes a crash when

Extend CI weight validation to check .bin files for corruption, not just safetensors. Models like BAAI/bge-m3 use .bin format, and corrupted cached files caused repeated test failures.

sglang.title: Fix swa memory pool size with spec
sglang.author: ispobock
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Previously, for some swa model like gpt-oss, if we use swa memory pool + spec, it will crash with oob in some kernels.
The root cause is that, the draft model is full, but the target model is hybrid swa/full. The draft model used the number of tokens before the swa ajustification as pool

vllm.title: [Perf] Cache exc.errors() result in validation exception handler

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Cache the result of exc.errors() to avoid calling it multiple times in the validation_exception_handler.
Before: exc.errors() called 3 times
After: Result cached in errors variable, used 3 times

Improves code readability

vllm.author: sjhddh
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Replace the fixed 0.5s sleep (marked with # FIXME: This test can be flaky) with a proper polling loop that waits for the response status to change before attempting to cancel.
Changes

Poll with 0.1s intervals until status changes from "queued" (max 5s)
Use proper assertions in the post-canc

vllm.title: [CPU Backend][BugFix] Fix failing Darwin pipelines
vllm.author: fadara01
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
PR #32869 updated CPU Backend to use torch 2.10 which caused Darwin pipelines to fail.
The root cause of the failure is:

uv uses build isolation by default
pyproject.toml is at torch 2.9.1, while cpu.txt is at 2.10.0
hence, vllm gets build against 2.9.1 but uses 2.10.0 at runtime

We should

Summary
This PR introduces further optimizations to the FP4 quantization kernels in vLLM, delivering substantial performance improvements across all tested shapes and batch sizes.
The main change is the use of 256-bit load.global instructions enabled via PTX 8.8, which significantly reduces global m
